articles.title: India's monsoon revives after two-week stall, heads into central belt
articles.description: India Monsoon: The southwest monsoon has resumed its advance after a two-week pause, with the IMD forecasting rains across central India this week, boosting kharif sowing and offering relief from the ongoing heatwave.

articles.title: Watch: Reliance Industries shares climb nearly 3% as Jio files for IPO
articles.description: Reliance Industries shares surged after Jio Platforms filed draft papers for what could become India's largest-ever IPO.The proposed offering could raise nearly ₹37,700 crore and value Jio Platforms at around $137 billion. At Reliance's 49th AGM, Mukesh Ambani said the Jio IPO would showcase India's ability to build technology companies of global scale.

articles.title: The Truth About FII Selling In India: Expert Analysis On The Upcoming Market Rebound
articles.description: Foreign institutional investors have remained heavy sellers in Indian markets over the past several months, but the outlook may be turning more constructive. According to Sandeep Tandon, Founder & CIO, Quant Mutual Fund, India's underperformance has been more relative than absolute, as global investors chased opportunities linked to the AI boom and technology-led rallies in other markets. However, Tandon believes the current phase could represent an important inflection point. He noted that several global markets, particularly Korea and Taiwan, are showing signs of topping out and may enter a period of correction or consolidation. If that happens, capital flows could gradually shift back toward India. Tandon views the recent pessimism toward Indian equities as an opportunity rather than a risk, arguing that market sentiment may be closer to a turning point than many investors realize.

articles.title: 'HOPE U.S. IS SERIOUS': Iranian Official Makes BIG Announcement On Swiss Peace Talks | WATCH
articles.description: Iran on Monday announced that a major progress had been made in the ongoing negotiations with the United States, highlighting new agreements aimed at stabilizing the situation in Lebanon and ensuring maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking about the latest round of talks, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei said a new mechanism known as the “De-confliction Cell” has been established with the involvement of mediators to monitor and preserve the Lebanon ceasefire. The Iranian official said a new mechanism known as the “De-confliction Cell” has been established with the involvement of mediators to monitor and preserve the Lebanon ceasefire. He also announced that an agreement had been reached on a mechanism to guarantee the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most strategically important waterways.

articles.title: “At 52, I left my husband; what my daughter revealed next left me...": How a mother finally broke free from years of abuse and chose peace over pain
articles.description: After enduring nearly two decades of abuse, Baljinder Kaur, at 52, finally left a marriage that had caused immense suffering. Driven by a desire to protect her daughter, she remarried at 33, only to face years of emotional and physical mistreatment. A heartbreaking revelation about her daughter's abuse became her breaking point.

articles.title: 'Maa Inti Bangaram' box office collection Day 4 [LIVE]: Samantha Ruth Prabhu starrer eyes Rs 28–30 crore net by week's end after strong opening weekend run
articles.description: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaram' delivered a rare feat over its opening weekend, three consecutive days of growth rather than the usual weekend plateau. After a Rs. 5.35 crore Day 1, the film jumped 43% on Day 2 to Rs. 7.65 crore and a further 32% on Day 3 to Rs. 10.10 crore, taking its three-day domestic net to Rs. 23.10 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 41.79 crore. As the film enters its first Monday, live tracking shows Rs. 0.82 crore collected so far across 1,236 shows, split Rs 0.77 crore in Telugu and Rs. 0.05 crore in Tamil, pushing the cumulative domestic net to Rs. 23.92 crore. With momentum building with each day, B.V. Nandini Reddy's female-led action-family drama looks well positioned for a strong first week.

The project has an estimated Gross Development Value (GDV) of approximately ₹5,600 crore with a development potential of around 1.8 million square feet. articles.title: 'Won't Let It Happen': Netanyahu Breaks Up With Trump? 'Iran Nuclear' Vow Stuns US Amid Talks
articles.description: As US-Iran negotiations continue in Switzerland, Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu has drawn a firm red line, declaring that Iran will never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons. He also insisted Israeli forces will remain in southern Lebanon despite growing international pressure. Netanyahu said no diplomatic breakthrough would change Israel's security policy, signaling potential friction as Washington pushes for a broader regional deal. His remarks come at a critical moment for Middle East diplomacy.

articles.title: Shimla School Administrator Murder: Chilling CCTV Footage Captures Masked Gunmen
articles.description: CCTV footage showing the fatal shooting of Shimla school administrator Meenakshi Mittal has surfaced online, revealing two masked men opening fire before fleeing. Police said Mittal was shot three times in the head. Two alleged shooters have been arrested and remanded to seven days' police custody.

articles.title: India’s first Polar & Ocean Museum to come up at NCPOR campus in Goa
articles.description: India is set to unveil its first Polar & Ocean Museum in Goa, a collaborative effort between Creative Museum Designers and the National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research. This state-of-the-art facility will showcase India's polar and ocean research through immersive, interactive exhibits, utilizing advanced technologies like VR and AR. The museum aims to educate the public on critical scientific expeditions and the significance of these extreme environments.

articles.title: Jaipur: Notorious burglar Satpal Fauji, associate arrested for theft
articles.description: Jaipur Police arrested notorious history-sheeter Satpal Fauji and an associate for daytime burglaries. Fauji, with 66 prior cases, ran an interstate gang and used silicone masks and wigs to avoid being identified by police.

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 re-exam: Candidates arrive amid tight security in K'taka
articles.description: Candidates arrived at centres in Davanagere, Karnataka, for the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination amid tight police security. A total of 7,395 candidates are set to appear for the exam across 13 centres in the district on Sunday.

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 re-exam begins in Thoothukudi amid tight security
articles.description: The NEET-UG 2026 re-examination began in Thoothukudi on Sunday with extensive security measures, including biometric verification and jammers. The NTA is conducting the exam for over 22.79 lakh candidates across India and abroad.

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 Re-exam: Tight security in Gujarat, Chandigarh, UP
articles.description: Elaborate security arrangements, including police personnel and surveillance teams, have been deployed in Gujarat, Chandigarh, and Uttar Pradesh for the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination to ensure its smooth and fair conduct on Sunday.

articles.title: NEET-2026 re-exam: State-level NTA control room activated in Dehradun
articles.description: Uttarakhand Police activated a state-level NTA control room in Dehradun to ensure the fair and transparent conduct of the NEET-2026 re-examination. Strict security measures are in place for over 6,000 candidates appearing at 16 centres.

articles.title: NEET-UG Re-exam: Parent urges better management to reduce stress
articles.description: In Visakhapatnam, a parent of a NEET-UG re-exam candidate urged the government for better exam management, citing recurring issues causing stress and financial strain on students and families. He appealed for corrective measures to prevent future problems.

articles.title: NEET-UG row: Nagpur student gets Abu Dhabi centre, NTA re-allots
articles.description: A Nagpur-based NEET-UG re-exam candidate was allotted a centre in Abu Dhabi, prompting an outcry. The NTA swiftly re-allotted a centre in Nagpur but later stated that their records show the student had chosen Abu Dhabi during the correction window.

articles.title: Ram temple donations: VHP calls for probe, harshest punishment
articles.description: VHP's Alok Kumar has called for a thorough SIT probe into the alleged irregularities in Ram temple donations, demanding the harshest punishment for anyone found guilty. He said the probe's outcome should be awaited without maligning everyone.
